name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
وَلُوطًا إِذْ قَالَ لِقَوْمِهِۦٓ أَتَأْتُونَ ٱلْفَـٰحِشَةَ وَأَنتُمْ تُبْصِرُونَ (٥٤)
```

```english
[We also sent] Lot, when he said to his people, ‘What! Do you commit this indecency while you look on? (54)
```

```arabic
أَئِنَّكُمْ لَتَأْتُونَ ٱلرِّجَالَ شَهْوَةً مِّن دُونِ ٱلنِّسَآءِ ۚ بَلْ أَنتُمْ قَوْمٌ تَجْهَلُونَ (٥٥)
```

```english
Do you approach men with [sexual] desire instead of women?! Indeed, you are a senseless lot!’ (55)
```

```arabic
۞ فَمَا كَانَ جَوَابَ قَوْمِهِۦٓ إِلَّآ أَن قَالُوٓا۟ أَخْرِجُوٓا۟ ءَالَ لُوطٍ مِّن قَرْيَتِكُمْ ۖ إِنَّهُمْ أُنَاسٌ يَتَطَهَّرُونَ (٥٦)
```

```english
But the only answer of his people was that they said, ‘Expel Lot’s family from your town! They are indeed a puritanical lot.’ (56)
```

```arabic
فَأَنجَيْنَـٰهُ وَأَهْلَهُۥٓ إِلَّا ٱمْرَأَتَهُۥ قَدَّرْنَـٰهَا مِنَ ٱلْغَـٰبِرِينَ (٥٧)
```

```english
So We delivered him and his family, except his wife. We ordained her to be among those who remained behind. (57)
```

```arabic
وَأَمْطَرْنَا عَلَيْهِم مَّطَرًا ۖ فَسَآءَ مَطَرُ ٱلْمُنذَرِينَ (٥٨)
```

```english
Then We poured down upon them a rain [of stones]. Evil was that rain for those who had been warned! (58)
```

## (Commentary)

This is a summary of the story of Lot (عليه‌السلام), which, like the preceding story, is characterized by the predominance of warning (الإنذار) over glad tidings (التبشير).

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَلُوطًا إِذْ قَالَ لِقَوْمِهِ أَتَأْتُونَ الْفَاحِشَةَ وَأَنْتُمْ تُبْصِرُونَ** "And Lot, when he said to his people, ‘Do you commit this indecency while you look on?’" (27:54). This is coordinated (معطوف) with the position of "We sent" (أرسلنا) in the previous story by means of an implied verb; the estimated meaning being: "And We certainly sent Lot." This has been suggested, though it is also possible that it is coordinated with the core of the narrative by implying the command "Mention" (اذكر). The term **indecency** (الفاحشة) refers to a trait reaching the utmost limit of repulsiveness (الشناعة), and what is intended here is sodomy.

His saying: **وَأَنْتُمْ تُبْصِرُونَ** "while you look on" means: while you are in a state where you see one another and look at one another during the act of indecency. This is similar to His saying in another place: **وَتَأْتُونَ فِي نَادِيكُمُ الْمُنْكَرَ** "and commit abominations in your gatherings?" (29:29). It has also been said that it refers to the "sight of the heart" (إبصار القلب), meaning their knowledge of its repulsiveness, but this is far-fetched.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **أَئِنَّكُمْ لَتَأْتُونَ الرِّجَالَ شَهْوَةً مِنْ دُونِ النِّسَاءِ ۚ بَلْ أَنْتُمْ قَوْمٌ تَجْهَلُونَ** "Do you approach men with desire instead of women? Rather, you are a senseless people." (27:55). The interrogative (الاستفهام) here is for denunciation (الإنكار). The inclusion of the two instruments of emphasis—the particle *inna* (إن) and the *lam* (اللام)—within the interrogative sentence serves to indicate that the content of the sentence is so far-fetched (الاستبعاد) that no one would believe it. In any case, the sentence serves as an explanation (التفسير) of the "indecency."

His saying: **بَلْ أَنْتُمْ قَوْمٌ تَجْهَلُونَ** "Rather, you are a senseless people" means: you are persistent in ignorance (الجهل); there is no benefit in rebuking you or denouncing you, for you are not to be deterred. The use of the second-person address **تَجْهَلُونَ** "you are senseless" in place of the third-person "they are senseless" is an instance of placing the effect (المسبب) in the position of the cause (السبب), as if it were said: "Rather, they are a people who are ignorant, and thus you are acting ignorantly."

**His saying, the Exalted:** **فَمَا كَانَ جَوَابَ قَوْمِهِ إِلَّا أَنْ قَالُوا أَخْرِجُوا آلَ لُوطٍ مِنْ قَرْيَتِكُمْ ۖ إِنَّهُمْ أُنَاسٌ يَتَطَهَّرُونَ** "But the only answer of his people was that they said, ‘Expel Lot’s family from your town! They are indeed a purist lot.’" (27:56). That is, they keep themselves pure from this act; this was said by way of mockery (الاستهزاء).

**His saying, the Exalted:** **فَأَنْجَيْنَاهُ وَأَهْلَهُ إِلَّا امْرَأَتَهُ قَدَّرْنَاهَا مِنَ الْغَابِرِينَ** "So We delivered him and his family, except his wife: We ordained her to be among those who remained behind." (27:57). What is meant by "his family" (أهله) is the people of his household, due to His saying, the Exalted: **فَمَا وَجَدْنَا فِيهَا غَيْرَ بَيْتٍ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ** "but We did not find in it any but one house of Muslims." (51:36). His saying: **قَدَّرْنَاهَا مِنَ الْغَابِرِينَ** "We ordained her to be among those who remained behind" means: We made her among those who stayed behind to face the punishment.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَأَمْطَرْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ مَطَرًا ۖ فَسَاءَ مَطَرُ الْمُنْذَرِينَ** "And We rained down upon them a rain: evil was the rain of those who were warned." (27:58). What is meant by "rain" (المطر) is stones of baked clay (سجيل), due to His saying, the Exalted: **وَأَمْطَرْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ حِجَارَةً مِنْ سِجِّيلٍ** "and rained on them stones of baked clay." (15:74). Thus, His saying: **مَطَرًا** "a rain," by being indefinite (التنكير), indicates its specific nature (النوعية)—that is, We sent down upon them a rain of momentous significance.
